name: Jeollanam-do (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: the fields, mountains and forests of the southern province
the harvest, sunshine, and the abundance of the southern coast
the southern sea and the islands that line the coast

description: A green field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem in white, yellow and blue, depicting an abstracted flower-and-wave motif, with the wordmark 'JEONNAM' below.
history: South Jeolla Province (Jeollanam-do, also known as Jeonnam), occupying the south-western corner of the Korean peninsula and home to thousands of islands, has used a series of provincial flags. The current design centres on a multicolour emblem evoking the province's coastline, rice fields and the chrysanthemum, one of its symbolic flowers.

features.symbolism: the fields, mountains and forests of southern Korea
abstract flower-and-wave motif representing the southern sea and islands, the rice fields, and the chrysanthemum, a symbolic flower of the province
